level_signals: 7+ years in Information Security with significant program management experience
Expected to define and build programs, not just maintain them
Comp range $185,400–$278,000 USD consistent with senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years in Information Security, with significant program management experience in product or engineering contexts — ideally including roles where you defined and built programs, not just maintained them.
A strong foundation across a broad range of AI Governance, Risk, and Safety (AI GRS) implications, security, and compliance topics.
1-2+ years experience leveraging AI models to drive program efficiencies (e.g., automating risk assessment or compliance gap analysis).
Proven ability to communicate complex technical and regulatory topics and write strong technical compliance requirements for diverse audiences (engineers, lawyers, customers, and GTM).
Excellent organizational skills, including the ability to manage external contractors and vendors.
A customer-centric mindset with a drive for process simplification.

A builder's mindset and comfort with ambiguity — Harvey ships fast, the AI regulatory landscape is still being written, and this role requires both strong judgment and the ability to create structure where none yet exists.

scope.surface: Product Trust — security and compliance review processes, AI risk governance, audit/certification readiness, customer-facing security enablement
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS, AI platform for legal and professional services
primary_metrics_inferred: Security and compliance review cycle time and coverage
Number of features reviewed and risk narratives delivered
core_responsibilities: Manage the end-to-end security and compliance review process for new features, including intake / planning, resource engagement, performing risk analysis, documenting trade-off decisions, and ensuring critical requirements for information security and AI risks (model behavior, safety, regulatory exposure) are addressed.

Act as a representative for the Security team to cross-functional teams and leadership, synthesizing risk reviews and threat models into formal security risk narratives, and reports before major launches.
Collaborate with Product Security and Infrastructure Security teams to integrate new Product Trust requirements and drive improvements into engineering, release, and change management processes.

Develop customer-facing security and privacy enablement content (e.g., materials, FAQs, positioning), attend customer security review calls, and manage technical security escalations from major customers.
Lead audit and certification readiness projects for key features and teams.
Support and address other priority Trust, Risk, and Compliance initiatives as they are prioritized.

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and wide ($185,400–$278,000 base)
Clear reporting structure (Head of Trust) and cross-functional scope described
Well-articulated role responsibilities with concrete examples (e.g., risk narratives, audit readiness)
Company has strong PMF signals: 1500+ customers in 60+ countries, world-class investors
Rare and high-impact domain at intersection of AI security, compliance, and product trust
red_flags: Location not specified — unclear if remote, hybrid, or onsite; no cities listed
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed anywhere in the JD
No equity or benefits mentioned beyond base compensation range
AI regulatory landscape described as still being written — high ambiguity environment may not suit all candidates
No nice-to-haves listed, making it harder to assess flexibility in the ideal profile

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and 7+ years requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and customer references)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
equity_mentioned (inferred as false; not mentioned in JD)
ambiguities: The '1-2+ years experience leveraging AI models' requirement uses an unusual range format ('1-2+') that could mean anywhere from 1 to 2+ years minimum; treated as hard requirement with minimum_value of 1.

The phrase 'ideally including roles where you defined and built programs' in the 7+ years requirement reads as a strong preference embedded in a hard requirement; treated as part of the hard requirement rather than a separate nice-to-have.
No location information provided — could be remote, hybrid, or onsite; left as 'unclear'.
